rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY PEREZ et al VS. KLEEGE INDUSTRIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 2: CROSS DEFENDANT ANOTHER PLANET ENTERTAINMENT, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Cross-Defendant Another Planet Entertainment, LLC's motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the first, second, and third causes of action and denied as to the fourth cause of action. As to the first, second, and third causes of action, Cross-Defendant has produced evidence that it was a special employer of Plaintiff Perez. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Cross-Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 10-18; compare with Riley v. Southwest Marine, Inc. (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1242, 1250.) As a consequence of establishing a special employer relationship, the special employer is immune from a common law tort action. (Santa Cruz Poultry, Inc. v. Superior Court (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 575, 578.) As such, the burden shift to Cross-Complainant Kleege Industries, Inc. to demonstrate a triable issue of material fact exists. Cross-Defendant has not carried its burden as it has not produced sufficient evidence to controvert. As to the fourth cause of action, Cross-Defendant has not shifted the burden. Cross-Defendant has not produced prima facie evidence that Cross-Complainant has been (or will be) paid in full for any loss. (See UMF 22-24; compare with Patent Scaffolding Co. v. William Simpson Const. (1967) 256 Cal.App.2d 506, 508.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: MARVIN ROBINSON VS. RUKHSANA TASNEEM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rukhsana Tasneem'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Deem Admissions Requests As Admitted, Request For Attorney Fees, Costs And Sanctions Against Plaintiff Marvin Robinson And His Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT RUKHSANA TASNEEM'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And Deem Admissions Requests As Admitted, Request For Attorney Fees, Costs And Sanctions Against Plaintiff Marvin Robinson And His Attorney. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The parties did not agree to any stay of discovery while they engaged in settlement discussions; to the contrary, they expressly left discovery open. Plaintiff's discovery responses on October 31, 2013 were not timely. Defendant's Requests for Admission thus are deemed to be admitted as true. Plaintiff has waived objections to the Form Interrogatories and Special Interrogatories, and is ordered to serve verifications for his responses within 10 days of this signed order, if he has not already done so. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $1400 for preparation and filing of this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to katherinehuibonhoa@paulhastings.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RAY POON-PHANG SEET MD VS. MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Administrative Writ
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 6: PETITIONER RAY SEET'S Petition For Administrative Writ The petition for writ of mandate is denied. In light of all the circumstances and the record before the Board, Petitioner fails to establish that respondent abused its discretion in denying the motions to continue the administrative hearing. Petitioner was aware of prior counsel's illness and did not move for a continuance in a timely fashion. Petitioner's request to continue the hearing did not comply with Gov't Code § 11524(b) and was made a mere month before the administrative hearing. Petitioner declined Respondent's offer of a continuance conditioned on Petitioner suspending his practice of medicine pending the continued hearing. rulings.Case Title: ESPERANZA COTA ROMERO VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 24: DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Denied. Standing is not necessarily limited to those persons who have the statutory right to control disposition of the remains. Here Defendant was itself the party that is accused of disturbing the remains. (See Christensen v. Sup.Ct. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 868, 875.) For the first cause of action, there are triable issues of fact whether UCSF maintained the applicable standard of care in performing the autopsy as proffered by the declarations of Defendant's expert Dr. Rosenthal and Plaintiffs' expert Dr. Bronston. (Flowers v. Torrance Mem'l Hosp. Med. Ctr. (1994) 8 Cal.4th 992, 1001; Kelley v. Trunk (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 519, 524.) Further, there are triable issues of fact whether there was fraud, overreaching or excusable neglect to excuse Esperanza Cota Romero's execution of the Autopsy Permit without first reading the instrument before signing it. (Stewart v. Preston Pipeline Inc. (2005) 134 Cal.App.4th 1565, 1588.) For the second cause of action, Defendant has not met its initial burden to demonstrate that as a public entity, it is immune from liability based on a cause of action for fraud with respect to conducting an autopsy given case law limiting the immunity to instances of financial or commercial fraud. (Gov. Code § 818.8; 5 Witkin, Cal. Summary (10th ed. 2005) Torts, § 242, p. 398.) Plaintiffs' motion to strike Dr. Rosenthal's declaration is denied. The objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: TELEKENEX, INC. VS. GEORGE GEORGIOU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Order Setting Aside Order Denying Deft'S Mtn To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 11: DEFENDANT GEORGE GEORGIOU'S Motion For Order Setting Aside Order Denying Defendant's Motion To Set Aside Default Denied. Defendant has not established a sufficient basis for setting aside the September 6, 2013 Order based on his having failed to file a reply brief or appear at the hearing because he made an error concerning the calendaring of the motion he had filed. The provision for mandatory relief due to attorney mistake under CCP § 473(b) only applies to relief from a judgment of dismissal or default. (Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 681.) Defendant is seeking to set aside an order denying his motion to vacate the default, not a default judgment. In the Proposed Declaration of George Georgiou, Defendant states that he chose not to respond to the Complaint. ( 2-3.) The provision for discretionary relief under CCP § 473(b) requires that a party seeking relief "demonstrate that such mistake, inadvertence, or general neglect was excusable." (Zamora v. Clayborn Contracting Group, Inc. (2002) 28 Cal.4th 249, 258.) Defendant has not demonstrated that a reasonably prudent person would not have checked the tentative rulings or appeared at a hearing on a date that he set. Further, "conduct falling below the professional standard of care, such as failure to timely object or to properly advance an argument, is not therefore excusable." (Id.) There is no statutory basis for setting aside a default because a party chose not to respond to a complaint, let alone for simply failing to file a reply brief or appear at a hearing. There is no reason to think that if a new hearing were granted the result would be any different given the basis for the motion in the first place. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: WYDE VOICE LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al VS. GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltff'S Written Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTs GLOBAL IP SOLUTIONS INC. and GOOGLE INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Written Discovery Responses And Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. Counsel should be prepared to discuss the following issues. (1) Whether the parties stipulate to the appointment of a referee under CCP 638 to hear and determine all discovery motions, including the two motions on calendar for November 15, 2013, and disputes relevant to discovery, and to report findings and make recommendations. (2) Assuming a discovery referee will be appointed, either by stipulation of the parties or by the Court's own motion, whether dividing the cost of the referee equally between the parties is a fair and reasonable allocation. (3) Assuming a discovery referee will be appointed, either by stipulation of the parties or by the Court's own motion, who the parties propose be appointed. The parties shall meet and confer in advance of the hearing to see if they can agree on an acceptable referee. (4) Assuming a discovery referee will be appointed, either by stipulation of the parties or by the Court's own motion, whether the parties anticipate needing the use of court facilities or personnel. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Judicial Form Interrogatories Disc-001 & Disc-002 (Ccp 2030.300(A)(3); And Request For Monetary Sanctions (Ccp 2023.010(E) And 2023.030(A)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MARK WILSON's Motion To Compel Responses To Judicial Form Interrogatories Disc-001 & Disc-002 (CCP 2030.300(A)(3); And Request For Monetary Sanctions (CCP 2023.010(E) And 2023.030(A). Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear. The Court takes judicial notice of the November 8, 2013 docket entry: Notice of Entry of Order/Notice of Ruling Filed Granting Defendants' and Cross-Complainants' Special Motion To Strike 2nd Amended Complaint As Strategic Litigation Against Public Participation. Parties should be prepared to discuss the effect of this notice of entry of order on any stay of discovery under CCP 425.16(g) and the instant motion. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RICCI CORNELL VS. CORZAC INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO INTERVENE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 19: INTERVENOR CLARICE LACAU'S MOTION TO INTERVENE Granted. The proposed complaint in intervention shall be filed within 10 days of the entry of this order. Intervenor is entitled to this relief pursuant to Corp. Code § 1800. Plaintiff's request to strike the corporation's answer is denied as procedurally improper and substantively inappropriate. Both the corporation and intervenor are entitled to assert their positions in opposition to plaintiff's complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT VS. JDS BUILDERS GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, November 15, 2013, Line 20: DEFENDANT SCOTTSDALE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The Court sustains the demurrer without leave to amend under CCP 430.10(d). Even if the insurance policy provides first party coverage to Plaintiff, the complaint indicates that JDS has third party coverage. Allowing the litigation to proceed as presently configured violates Evid. Code § 1155. In addition, it is dubious whether JDS and Defendant are properly joined together because Plaintiff's claims against them do not arise out of the same transaction. See CCP 379. Plaintiff's claim against JDS arises from a defective paint job. Plaintiff's claim against Defendant arises from its alleged breach of a contractual duty to process/honor a first party insurance claim. Whatever claims Plaintiff may have against Defendant must be pursued in a separate action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)
